concerns.text: T013 (evaluate.py) is marked 'DEPENDS ON T012' (train.py), but the US1 goal requires comparing the adaptive policy against a static baseline. T012 only implements the adaptive loop. There is no explicit task to implement or load the static PICA baseline policy before T013 runs, creating a missing producer for the baseline data required by the paired t-test in T014.

T014 (analysis.py) performs a paired t-test, which requires paired data (same object, two policies). T013 (evaluate.py) and T014a (aggregate.py) do not explicitly mandate logging the 'object_id' alongside success rates to enable pairing. Without a task ensuring the data structure preserves object identity across runs, T014 cannot validly execute a paired test, violating the data-flow dependency for FR-005.

T007a executes the generator to produce geometry files, but T015 (integration) depends on these artifacts. However, T007 (generator class) and T007a (execution) are listed as parallel-safe [P] alongside T004-T009. If T007a runs before T007 is fully implemented or if the execution order isn't enforced, T015 will fail. The 'Producer before Consumer' rule requires T007a to be strictly sequential after T007, not parallel with other foundational tasks that don't consume its output.

T012b (verify no CUDA) and T028 (add FR-004 checks) are redundant verification tasks for the same constraint. T012b is placed in US1 implementation, while T028 is in US3. This scatters the enforcement of a foundational constraint (FR-004) across user stories, potentially leading to inconsistent enforcement if T028 is skipped. Consolidating this into a single foundational check or ensuring T012b is the primary gate would improve ordering clarity.
